title: wrightia viridiflora
intro: Wrightia viridiflora, also known as 'Vasaka' or 'Sariva', is a medicinal plant native to the tropical regions of Asia. It is a shrub or small tree with greenish-yellow flowers, valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which help to alleviate symptoms of cold, cough, and bronchitis. Wrightia viridiflora is used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, asthma, and skin infections.
